title: Lewis University Library AI Commons — Resource Schema
description: Metadata schema for resources in the Lewis University Library AI Commons. Each entry describes a single AI-assisted resource: an application, lesson plan, prompt library, policy document, assessment rubric, workflow, framework, or other resource.

id.description: URL-safe unique identifier matching the GitHub repo name. Lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only.

title.description: Human-readable resource title as it appears in the gallery.

description.description: A concise description of what the resource is and how it supports teaching, learning, research, or institutional operations. Aim for 2-4 sentences.

author.description: Name and optional title of the primary contributor.

department.description: Lewis University department or unit of the primary contributor.

ai_tool.description: The primary generative AI tool(s) used to create the resource. For multi-tool workflows, use 'Multiple' and list tools in parentheses.

format.description: The form or medium of the resource — what it IS, structurally.

content_type.description: The pedagogical or functional purpose of the resource — what it DOES.

blooms_level.description: Primary Bloom's Taxonomy level the resource targets. Optional for non-instructional resources.

date_submitted.description: Date the resource was submitted for review (YYYY-MM-DD).

live_url.description: URL where the resource is accessible. For applications, the deployed app. For documents, the hosted page or PDF.

source_url.description: URL of the source repository (typically GitHub).

tags.description: Lowercase hyphenated keywords for search and discovery.

screenshot.description: Relative path to a screenshot or preview image (PNG or WebP, 1200x800px recommended).

status.description: Publication status. Only 'published' resources appear in the public gallery.

course.description: Course number/name if the resource was built for a specific course.

license.description: License. Defaults to MIT for code, CC-BY-4.0 for documents.

ai_role.description: Brief description of how AI was used in creating this resource. Helps other contributors understand the human-AI collaboration pattern.
examples: Claude generated the full application code from a detailed prompt describing clinical scenarios.
ChatGPT drafted initial lesson plan structure; instructor revised extensively and added all discussion questions.
Claude wrote the policy framework; library team revised for institutional voice and legal compliance.
